title: शिवरक्षास्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री शिवरक्षास्तोत्रम् ॥

ॐ अस्य श्री शिवरक्षास्तोत्रस्य
याज्ञवल्क्य ऋषिः। श्रीसदाशिवो देवता।
अनुष्टुप् छन्दः। श्रीसदाशिवप्रीत्यर्थे शिवरक्षास्तोत्रजपे विनियोगः॥

चरितं देवदेवस्य महादेवस्य पावनम्।
अपारं परमोदारं चतुर्वर्गस्य साधनम्॥१॥

गौरीविनायकोपेतं पञ्चवक्त्रं त्रिनेत्रकम्।
शिवं ध्यात्वा दशभुजं शिवरक्षां पठेन्नरः॥२॥

गङ्गाधरः शिरः पातु भालमर्धेन्दुशेखरः।
नयने मदनध्वंसी कर्णौ सर्पविभूषणः॥३॥

घ्राणं पातु पुरारातिर्मुखं पातु जगत्पतिः।
जिह्वां वागीश्वरः पातु कन्धरां शितिकण्ठकः॥४॥

श्रीकण्ठः पातु मे कण्ठं स्कन्धौ विश्वधुरन्धरः।
भुजौ भूभारसंहर्ता करौ पातु पिनाकधृक्॥५॥

हृदयं शङ्करः पातु जठरं गिरिजापतिः।
नाभिं मृत्युञ्जयः पातु कटिं व्याघ्राजिनाम्बरः॥६॥

सक्थिनी पातु दीनार्तशरणागतवत्सलः।
ऊरू महेश्वरः पातु जानुनी जगदीश्वरः॥७॥

जङ्घे पातु जगत्कर्ता गुल्फौ पातु गणाधिपः।
चरणौ करुणासिन्धुः सर्वाङ्गानि सदाशिवः॥८॥

एतां शिवबलोपेतां रक्षां यः सुकृती पठेत्।
स भुक्त्वा सकलान् कामान् शिवसायुज्यमाप्नुयात्॥९॥

ग्रहभूतपिशाचाद्यैस्त्रासं नैवोपजायते।
भुक्तिं मुक्तिं च लभते शिवरक्षां पठेन्नरः॥१०॥

॥ इति श्री यज्ञवल्क्यकृतं शिवरक्षास्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Shiva Raksha Stotram ||

Om Asya Shri Shiva-raksha-stotrasya
Yajnavalkya Rishiph. Shri Sadashivo Devata.
Anushtup Chhandah. Shri Sadashiva-prityarthe Shiva-raksha-stotra-jape Viniyogah.

Charitam Deva-devasya Mahadevasya Pavanam.
Aparam Paramodaram Chaturvargasya Sadhanam.||1||

Gauri-vinayakopetam Panchavaktram Trinetrakam.
Shivam Dhyatva Dashabhujam Shiva-raksham Pathennnarah.||2||

Gangadharah Shirah Patu Bhalam-ardhendu-shekharah.
Nayane Madana-dhvamsi Karnau Sarpa-vibhushanah.||3||

Ghranam Patu Puraratir-mukham Patu Jagatpatih.
Jihvam Vagishvarah Patu Kandharam Shitikanthakah.||4||

Shrikanthah Patu Me Kantham Skandhau Vishva-dhurandharah.
Bhujau Bhubhara-samharta Karau Patu Pinakadhrik.||5||

Hridayam Shankarah Patu Jatharam Girijapatih.
Nabhim Mrityunjayah Patu Katim Vyaghrajinaambarah.||6||

Sakthini Patu Dinarta-sharanagata-vatsalah.
Uru Maheshvarah Patu Januni Jagadishvarah.||7||

Janghe Patu Jagatkarta Gulphau Patu Ganadhipah.
Charanau Karunasindhu Sarvangani Sadashivah.||8||

Etam Shiva-balopetam Raksham Yah Sukritee Pathet.
Sa Bhuktva Sakalan Kaman Shiva-sayujyam-apnuyat.||9||

Graha-bhuta-pishachadyais-trasam Naivopajayate.
Bhuktim Muktim Cha Labhate Shiva-raksham Pathennnarah.||10||

|| Iti Shri Yajnavalkya-kritam Shiva Raksha Stotram Sampoornam ||
hindiMeaning:
इस शिवरक्षा स्तोत्र के ऋषि याज्ञवल्क्य हैं, देवता सदाशिव हैं, छन्द अनुष्टुप् है।

१. देवदेव महादेव का पवित्र चरित्र, अपार, परम उदार, चतुर्वर्ग (धर्म, अर्थ, काम, मोक्ष) का साधन है।

२. गौरी-गणेश सहित, पञ्चमुख, त्रिनेत्र, दशभुज शिव का ध्यान करके शिवरक्षा का पाठ करे।

३. गंगाधर शिर की, अर्धेन्दुशेखर ललाट की, मदनध्वंसी नेत्रों की, सर्पविभूषण कानों की रक्षा करें।

४. पुरारि नासिका की, जगत्पति मुख की, वागीश्वर जिह्वा की, शितिकण्ठ कन्धे की रक्षा करें।

५. श्रीकण्ठ कण्ठ की, विश्वधुरन्धर स्कन्धों की, भूभारसंहर्ता भुजाओं की, पिनाकधारी हाथों की रक्षा करें।

६. शंकर हृदय की, गिरिजापति उदर की, मृत्युञ्जय नाभि की, व्याघ्रचर्मधारी कटि की रक्षा करें।

७. दीनार्तशरणागतवत्सल जंघों की, महेश्वर ऊरुओं की, जगदीश्वर घुटनों की रक्षा करें।

८. जगत्कर्ता पिण्डलियों की, गणाधिप टखनों की, करुणासिन्धु चरणों की और सदाशिव सर्वांगों की रक्षा करें।

९-१०. शिवबलयुक्त इस रक्षा का पाठ करने से सकल काम भोगकर शिवसायुज्य मिलता है। ग्रह-भूत-पिशाच आदि का भय नहीं होता। भुक्ति और मुक्ति दोनों प्राप्त होती हैं।

viniyog.shloka: ॐ अस्य श्रीशिवरक्षास्तोत्रमन्त्रस्य याज्ञवल्क्य ऋषिः। अनुष्टुप् छन्दः। श्रीसदाशिवो देवता। ॐ नमः शिवाय बीजम्। शिवरक्षाशक्तिः शक्तिः। सर्वाङ्गरक्षार्थे शिवरक्षास्तोत्रजपे विनियोगः॥

benefits: Complete body protection (Kavach-style) — each body part protected by Shiva's names
Composed by the great sage Yajnavalkya — extremely authentic and powerful
Traditionally recited for removal of fear of grahas, bhutas, pishacha, and evil forces
Invokes both bhukti (worldly enjoyment) and mukti (liberation)
Traditionally recited for fulfillment of desires and leads to shiva sayujya
Powerful daily protection prayer for Shiva devotees
description: Among the most cherished prayers to Lord Shiva, Shiva Raksha Stotram (शिवरक्षास्तोत्रम्) stands as a devotional hymn of praise. This sacred composition is sourced from Composed by Yajnavalkya Rishi and continues to inspire devotees worldwide. Regular recitation is believed to remove fear of Grahas, Bhutas, Pishacha, and evil forces, composed by the great sage Yajnavalkya — extremely authentic and powerful, and complete body protection (Kavach-style) — each body part protected by Shiva's names. This prayer is traditionally recited on Monday (Somvar) and during Maha Shivaratri and Shravan month to invoke divine grace. Comprising 10 verses, it can be completed in approximately 6 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Shiva Raksha Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Shiva Raksha Stotram by Yajnavalkya in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Complete body protection prayer invoking Lord Shiva's divine shield.

seoFaqs.question: Who is the seer (rishi) of the Shiva Raksha Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: Tradition records that the rishi (seer) of the Shiva Raksha Stotram is Yajnavalkya, the same Vedic sage associated with the White Yajurveda recension and the Brihadaranyaka Upanishad. Saivism.net, Drikpanchang and Bhaktinidhi all preserve this attribution in the standard viniyoga (ritual prelude) of the stotram, which states 'asya Sri Shiva Raksha Stotra mantrasya Yajnavalkya rishih, anushtup chhandah, Sri Sadashivo devata'. The traditional account holds that Shiva himself revealed the verses to Yajnavalkya in a dream, after which the sage transmitted them as a protective recitation. The chhanda (metre) is anushtup, the standard 8-syllable-quarter Vedic metre, which makes the stotram easy to chant and memorise.

seoFaqs.question: What is the protective structure of the Shiva Raksha Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: The stotram is a kavacha-style protective hymn in which Shiva is invoked to guard each part of the devotee's body, head, forehead, eyes, ears, nose, mouth, throat, shoulders, arms, chest, stomach, navel, hips, thighs, knees, feet and so on, with each body part assigned to a specific Shiva epithet. For example, 'let he who carries Ganga protect my head, let he who keeps the crescent moon protect my forehead, let the destroyer of Kama protect my eyes, let he who wears serpents as ornaments protect my ears.' This systematic anga-nyasa (limb-assignment) is characteristic of kavacha hymns and creates a verbal armour that the devotee carries through the day. The structure is parallel to the Vishnu Sahasranama Phalashruti and the Devi Kavacha.

seoFaqs.question: How does the Shiva Raksha Stotram differ from the Shiva Kavacha?
seoFaqs.answer: Both are protective hymns, but they are distinct compositions. The Shiva Raksha Stotram, attributed to Yajnavalkya, is a shorter anushtup-metre hymn organised around body-part protection, generally 17 to 23 verses depending on the recension. The Shiva Kavacha (sometimes called Rudra Kavacha) is a longer composition preserved in the Skanda Purana, organised as a more elaborate ritual armour with mantric sequences and beeja syllables. Devotees often chant both, treating the Raksha Stotram as a daily nitya-paath and the Kavacha as a weekly or monthly fortification. Some lineages classify the Raksha Stotram as a beginner-friendly entry point and the Kavacha as a more advanced, initiation-aligned recitation requiring stricter procedural observance.

seoFaqs.question: When is the Shiva Raksha Stotram traditionally recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The hymn is recited every morning after the bath as the devotee's first protective invocation, before stepping out for the day's activities. It is also chanted on Mondays, Pradosham evenings, during Shravana and Karthika months, and on Maha Shivaratri. Many Shaiva householders include it in the bedtime sequence as well, framing the day with morning and evening protective recitations. In times of illness, fear, travel anxiety or perceived spiritual disturbance, intensified recitation (3, 7 or 11 rounds) is recommended. The standard posture is seated facing east or north before a Shiva image or lingam, with vibhuti applied. The entire stotram takes approximately 4 to 6 minutes to recite at moderate tempo, making daily practice highly feasible.

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the closing phala-shruti promise?
seoFaqs.answer: The closing verses of the Shiva Raksha Stotram promise that those who recite this protective hymn with devotion will have all their dharmic desires fulfilled, will attain proximity to Shiva after death, and that planetary afflictions, ghosts, ghouls and other negative influences (graha, bhuta, preta, pisacha) operating in the three worlds will retreat from them. Bhaktinidhi and Saivism.net translations preserve this phala-shruti uniformly. Shaiva commentators emphasise that the protection works at three levels: physical safety from accidents and illness, mental protection from fear and anxiety, and karmic protection from invisible negative influences. The protection is understood to be sustained throughout the day by the recitation in the morning, which is why daily practice is considered more effective than occasional intensive recitation.

seoFaqs.question: Are there any prerequisites for chanting the Shiva Raksha Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: No formal initiation, fasting or elaborate purity rule is required. The stotram is treated as a sarva-adhikara recitation, meaning it is open to all devotees regardless of varna, gender, age or initiation status. Standard preparations are minimal: bath or hand-foot wash, clean clothes, lit lamp, vibhuti or chandana mark, and seated posture facing east or north. Pronunciation accuracy is encouraged, especially for the anushtup metre which lends itself to rhythmic chanting, and beginners are advised to start with audio recordings such as those by Hari Om Sharan or temple-affiliated chanters. Children can be taught the hymn from age 7 onwards as a foundational protective recitation, building familiarity with Sanskrit and a daily devotional habit.
